<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和倉温泉お祭り会館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和倉温泉お祭り会館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q97274614]] Wikidata name: Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencing style|date=May 2021}} {{Museum | name = Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall | native_name = 和倉温泉お祭り会館 | native_name_lang = ja | image = | former_name = Wakura Onsen Tourism Hall | focus = Folklore, History | director = | owner = [[:en:Nanao City|Nanao City]] | operator = Nanao-Nakanoto DMO | established = [[June 1, 2020]] | location = 13-1 Wakuramachi 2-bu, Nanao City, [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|5|11.67|N|136|55|7.49|E|display=inline,title}} | website = https://wakura-omatsuri.com/ }}
'''Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall''' (和倉温泉お祭り会館, ''Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Kaikan'') is a cultural museum in [[:en:Nanao City|Nanao City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. Located in the famous [[:en:Wakura Onsen|Wakura Onsen]] hot spring area, the museum showcases the city's four major traditional festivals, including large-scale [[:en:Matsuri float|Yatai (festival floats)]] and other festival artifacts.
== Overview == Nanao City, known for its numerous traditional festivals, decided to establish the museum following the [[:en:2016|2016]] inclusion of the {{ill|Seihakusai Hikiyama Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai Hikiyama Festival|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}} as a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:Intangible Cultural Heritage|Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The facility was developed by renovating the aging 1981 Wakura Onsen Tourism Hall, addressing structural issues and converting the main hall into an exhibition space.<ref name="Hokkoku">Hokkoku Shimbun, June 1, 2020.</ref><ref name="Hokuriku">Hokuriku Chunichi Shimbun, June 1, 2020.</ref><ref name="Furusato">[https://www.furusato-tax.jp/gcf/493 Furusato Tax Project for Festival Preservation]</ref>
The facility highlights four key festivals: * [[Seihakusai]] - designated as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property * [[Okuma Kabuto Festival]] - also a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property * [[Mukōda Fire Festival]] - recognized as a [[Folk Cultural Property|Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the "Noto Kiriko Festivals" * [[Ishizaki Hōtō Festival]] - part of the "Noto Kiriko Festivals"
Initially slated for an April 25, 2020, opening, the launch was delayed due to the COVID-19 pandemic.<ref name="Hokkoku"/>
== Features == === Exhibition Hall (Paid Admission)=== The former 1,100-seat main hall has been transformed into a 17-meter-high exhibition space displaying full-scale festival floats and artifacts.<ref>[http://www.ryoko-net.co.jp/?p=78090 Ryoko Shimbun]</ref> Exhibits include: * Seihakusai's "Dekayama" - a new full-scale replica created as a fourth float for permanent display. * Okuma Kabuto's Waku Bata - 16 meters high. * Mukōda Fire Festival’s Ōtaimatsu - represented by a 15-meter banner due to the original’s 30-meter height. * Ishizaki Hōtō’s Daifutō - 13 meters high.
=== Festival Theater and Interactive Zone === The theater features a 16.2-meter-wide screen showing immersive festival footage. Interactive experiences allow visitors to "participate" in festivals through projections and simulations.<ref name="Hokuriku"/>
=== Tourism Information Entrance (Free Zone) === The entrance area features: * Miniature and small-scale exhibits of local floats. * Tourism information and guides. * A gift shop offering festival-themed souvenirs.
=== Additional Facilities === * Meeting rooms and Japanese-style rooms (available for rent). * Cooking practice rooms.
== Visitor Information == * Open: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM) * Closed: Second and fourth Wednesdays (or following day if a holiday) and during New Year’s holidays. * Admission: Adults ¥800, children ¥400, infants free. * Free parking available for buses and private vehicles.
== Access == * '''By car''': ~6 minutes from [[Wakura IC]] on the [[Noto Satoyama Kaidō]]. * '''By train''': ~20 minutes on foot or 6 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]]. * '''By bus''': Immediate access from "Omatsuri Kaikan" bus stop (Hokutetsu Noto Bus).
== Surrounding Attractions == * [[:en:Wakura Onsen|Wakura Onsen]] * Wakura Onsen Sōyu Public Bath * [[Wakura Showa Museum and Toy Museum]] * [[Isolite Diatomite Memorial Museum]] * [[Yoshihiro Tsujiguchi Art Museum]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official website|https://wakura-omatsuri.com/|Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和倉温泉お祭り会館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和倉温泉お祭り会館]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:和倉温泉お祭り会館]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97274614]] Wikidata name: Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:32, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencing style|date=May 2021}} {{Museum | name = Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall | native_name = 和倉温泉お祭り会館 | native_name_lang = ja | image = | former_name = Wakura Onsen Tourism Hall | focus = Folklore, History | director = | owner = [[:en:Nanao City|Nanao City]] | operator = Nanao-Nakanoto DMO | established = [[June 1, 2020]] | location = 13-1 Wakuramachi 2-bu, Nanao City, [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|5|11.67|N|136|55|7.49|E|display=inline,title}} | website = https://wakura-omatsuri.com/ }}
'''Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall''' (和倉温泉お祭り会館, ''Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Kaikan'') is a cultural museum in [[:en:Nanao City|Nanao City]], [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]], Japan. Located in the famous [[:en:Wakura Onsen|Wakura Onsen]] hot spring area, the museum showcases the city's four major traditional festivals, including large-scale [[:en:Matsuri float|Yatai (festival floats)]] and other festival artifacts.
== Overview == Nanao City, known for its numerous traditional festivals, decided to establish the museum following the [[:en:2016|2016]] inclusion of the {{ill|Seihakusai Hikiyama Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai Hikiyama Festival|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}} as a [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:Intangible Cultural Heritage|Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The facility was developed by renovating the aging 1981 Wakura Onsen Tourism Hall, addressing structural issues and converting the main hall into an exhibition space.<ref name="Hokkoku">Hokkoku Shimbun, June 1, 2020.</ref><ref name="Hokuriku">Hokuriku Chunichi Shimbun, June 1, 2020.</ref><ref name="Furusato">[https://www.furusato-tax.jp/gcf/493 Furusato Tax Project for Festival Preservation]</ref>
The facility highlights four key festivals: * [[Seihakusai]] - designated as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property * [[Okuma Kabuto Festival]] - also a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property * [[Mukōda Fire Festival]] - recognized as a [[Folk Cultural Property|Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the "Noto Kiriko Festivals" * [[Ishizaki Hōtō Festival]] - part of the "Noto Kiriko Festivals"
Initially slated for an April 25, 2020, opening, the launch was delayed due to the COVID-19 pandemic.<ref name="Hokkoku"/>
== Features == === Exhibition Hall (Paid Admission)=== The former 1,100-seat main hall has been transformed into a 17-meter-high exhibition space displaying full-scale festival floats and artifacts.<ref>[http://www.ryoko-net.co.jp/?p=78090 Ryoko Shimbun]</ref> Exhibits include: * Seihakusai's "Dekayama" - a new full-scale replica created as a fourth float for permanent display. * Okuma Kabuto's Waku Bata - 16 meters high. * Mukōda Fire Festival’s Ōtaimatsu - represented by a 15-meter banner due to the original’s 30-meter height. * Ishizaki Hōtō’s Daifutō - 13 meters high.
=== Festival Theater and Interactive Zone === The theater features a 16.2-meter-wide screen showing immersive festival footage. Interactive experiences allow visitors to "participate" in festivals through projections and simulations.<ref name="Hokuriku"/>
=== Tourism Information Entrance (Free Zone) === The entrance area features: * Miniature and small-scale exhibits of local floats. * Tourism information and guides. * A gift shop offering festival-themed souvenirs.
=== Additional Facilities === * Meeting rooms and Japanese-style rooms (available for rent). * Cooking practice rooms.
== Visitor Information == * Open: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM) * Closed: Second and fourth Wednesdays (or following day if a holiday) and during New Year’s holidays. * Admission: Adults ¥800, children ¥400, infants free. * Free parking available for buses and private vehicles.
== Access == * '''By car''': ~6 minutes from [[Wakura IC]] on the [[Noto Satoyama Kaidō]]. * '''By train''': ~20 minutes on foot or 6 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]]. * '''By bus''': Immediate access from "Omatsuri Kaikan" bus stop (Hokutetsu Noto Bus).
== Surrounding Attractions == * [[:en:Wakura Onsen|Wakura Onsen]] * Wakura Onsen Sōyu Public Bath * [[Wakura Showa Museum and Toy Museum]] * [[Isolite Diatomite Memorial Museum]] * [[Yoshihiro Tsujiguchi Art Museum]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official website|https://wakura-omatsuri.com/|Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall}}
{{translated page|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|version=102277377|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall''' (和倉温泉お祭り会館, ''Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Kaikan'') is a cultural museum in [[:en:Nanao City|Nanao City]], {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. Located in the famous {{ill|Wakura Onsen|en|Wakura Onsen|WD=Q7961227|lt=Wakura Onsen}} hot spring area, the museum showcases the city's four major traditional festivals, including large-scale {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Yatai (festival floats)}} and other festival artifacts.
== Overview == Nanao City, known for its numerous traditional festivals, decided to establish the museum following the {{ill|2016|en|2016|WD=Q25245|lt=2016}} inclusion of the {{ill|Seihakusai Hikiyama Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai Hikiyama Festival|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}} as a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} [[:en:Intangible Cultural Heritage|Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The facility was developed by renovating the aging 1981 Wakura Onsen Tourism Hall, addressing structural issues and converting the main hall into an exhibition space.<ref name="Hokkoku">Hokkoku Shimbun, June 1, 2020.</ref><ref name="Hokuriku">Hokuriku Chunichi Shimbun, June 1, 2020.</ref><ref name="Furusato">[https://www.furusato-tax.jp/gcf/493 Furusato Tax Project for Festival Preservation]</ref>
The facility highlights four key festivals: * [[Seihakusai]] - designated as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property * [[Okuma Kabuto Festival]] - also a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property * [[Mukōda Fire Festival]] - recognized as a [[Folk Cultural Property|Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the "Noto Kiriko Festivals" * [[Ishizaki Hōtō Festival]] - part of the "Noto Kiriko Festivals"
Initially slated for an April 25, 2020, opening, the launch was delayed due to the COVID-19 pandemic.<ref name="Hokkoku"/>
== Features == === Exhibition Hall (Paid Admission)=== The former 1,100-seat main hall has been transformed into a 17-meter-high exhibition space displaying full-scale festival floats and artifacts.<ref>[http://www.ryoko-net.co.jp/?p=78090 Ryoko Shimbun]</ref> Exhibits include: * Seihakusai's "Dekayama" - a new full-scale replica created as a fourth float for permanent display. * Okuma Kabuto's Waku Bata - 16 meters high. * Mukōda Fire Festival’s Ōtaimatsu - represented by a 15-meter banner due to the original’s 30-meter height. * Ishizaki Hōtō’s Daifutō - 13 meters high.
=== Festival Theater and Interactive Zone === The theater features a 16.2-meter-wide screen showing immersive festival footage. Interactive experiences allow visitors to "participate" in festivals through projections and simulations.<ref name="Hokuriku"/>
=== Tourism Information Entrance (Free Zone) === The entrance area features: * Miniature and small-scale exhibits of local floats. * Tourism information and guides. * A gift shop offering festival-themed souvenirs.
=== Additional Facilities === * Meeting rooms and Japanese-style rooms (available for rent). * Cooking practice rooms.
== Visitor Information == * Open: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM) * Closed: Second and fourth Wednesdays (or following day if a holiday) and during New Year’s holidays. * Admission: Adults ¥800, children ¥400, infants free. * Free parking available for buses and private vehicles.
== Access == * '''By car''': ~6 minutes from [[Wakura IC]] on the [[Noto Satoyama Kaidō]]. * '''By train''': ~20 minutes on foot or 6 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]]. * '''By bus''': Immediate access from "Omatsuri Kaikan" bus stop (Hokutetsu Noto Bus).
== Surrounding Attractions == * {{ill|Wakura Onsen|en|Wakura Onsen|WD=Q7961227|lt=Wakura Onsen}} * Wakura Onsen Sōyu Public Bath * [[Wakura Showa Museum and Toy Museum]] * [[Isolite Diatomite Memorial Museum]] * [[Yoshihiro Tsujiguchi Art Museum]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official website|https://wakura-omatsuri.com/|Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall}}
{{translated page|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|version=102277377|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall''' (和倉温泉お祭り会館, ''Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Kaikan'') is a cultural museum in [[:en:Nanao City|Nanao City]], {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, Japan. Located in the famous {{ill|Wakura Onsen|en|Wakura Onsen|WD=Q7961227|lt=Wakura Onsen}} hot spring area, the museum showcases the city's four major traditional festivals, including large-scale {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Yatai (festival floats)}} and other festival artifacts.
== Overview == Nanao City, known for its numerous traditional festivals, decided to establish the museum following the {{ill|2016|en|2016|WD=Q25245|lt=2016}} inclusion of the {{ill|Seihakusai Hikiyama Festival|ja|青柏祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihakusai Hikiyama Festival|1=Seihaku Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661913}} as a {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} [[:en:Intangible Cultural Heritage|Intangible Cultural Heritage]]. The facility was developed by renovating the aging 1981 Wakura Onsen Tourism Hall, addressing structural issues and converting the main hall into an exhibition space.<ref name="Hokkoku">Hokkoku Shimbun, June 1, 2020.</ref><ref name="Hokuriku">Hokuriku Chunichi Shimbun, June 1, 2020.</ref><ref name="Furusato">[https://www.furusato-tax.jp/gcf/493 Furusato Tax Project for Festival Preservation]</ref>
The facility highlights four key festivals: * [[Seihakusai]] - designated as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property * [[Okuma Kabuto Festival]] - also a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property * [[Mukōda Fire Festival]] - recognized as a [[Folk Cultural Property|Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the "Noto Kiriko Festivals" * [[Ishizaki Hōtō Festival]] - part of the "Noto Kiriko Festivals"
Initially slated for an April 25, 2020, opening, the launch was delayed due to the COVID-19 pandemic.<ref name="Hokkoku"/>
== Features == === Exhibition Hall (Paid Admission)=== The former 1,100-seat main hall has been transformed into a 17-meter-high exhibition space displaying full-scale festival floats and artifacts.<ref>[http://www.ryoko-net.co.jp/?p=78090 Ryoko Shimbun]</ref> Exhibits include: * Seihakusai's "Dekayama" - a new full-scale replica created as a fourth float for permanent display. * Okuma Kabuto's Waku Bata - 16 meters high. * Mukōda Fire Festival’s Ōtaimatsu - represented by a 15-meter banner due to the original’s 30-meter height. * Ishizaki Hōtō’s Daifutō - 13 meters high.
=== Festival Theater and Interactive Zone === The theater features a 16.2-meter-wide screen showing immersive festival footage. Interactive experiences allow visitors to "participate" in festivals through projections and simulations.<ref name="Hokuriku"/>
=== Tourism Information Entrance (Free Zone) === The entrance area features: * Miniature and small-scale exhibits of local floats. * Tourism information and guides. * A gift shop offering festival-themed souvenirs.
=== Additional Facilities === * Meeting rooms and Japanese-style rooms (available for rent). * Cooking practice rooms.
== Visitor Information == * Open: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM) * Closed: Second and fourth Wednesdays (or following day if a holiday) and during New Year’s holidays. * Admission: Adults ¥800, children ¥400, infants free. * Free parking available for buses and private vehicles.
== Access == * '''By car''': ~6 minutes from [[Wakura IC]] on the [[Noto Satoyama Kaidō]]. * '''By train''': ~20 minutes on foot or 6 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]]. * '''By bus''': Immediate access from "Omatsuri Kaikan" bus stop (Hokutetsu Noto Bus).
== Surrounding Attractions == * {{ill|Wakura Onsen|en|Wakura Onsen|WD=Q7961227|lt=Wakura Onsen}} * Wakura Onsen Sōyu Public Bath * [[Wakura Showa Museum and Toy Museum]] * [[Isolite Diatomite Memorial Museum]] * [[Yoshihiro Tsujiguchi Art Museum]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official website|https://wakura-omatsuri.com/|Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall}}
{{translated page|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|version=102277377|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Located in the famous {{ill|Wakura Onsen|en|Wakura Onsen|WD=Q7961227|lt=Wakura Onsen|1=Wakura Onsen}} hot spring area, the museum showcases the city's four major traditional festivals, including large-scale {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Yatai (festival floats)|1=Matsuri float}} and other festival artifacts.

The facility was developed by renovating the aging 1981 Wakura Onsen Tourism Hall, addressing structural issues and converting the main hall into an exhibition space.<ref name="Hokkoku">Hokkoku Shimbun, June 1, 2020.</ref><ref name="Hokuriku">Hokuriku Chunichi Shimbun, June 1, 2020.</ref><ref name="Furusato">[https://www.furusato-tax.jp/gcf/493 Furusato Tax Project for Festival Preservation]</ref>
The facility highlights four key festivals: * [[Seihakusai]] - designated as a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property * [[Okuma Kabuto Festival]] - also a National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property * [[Mukōda Fire Festival]] - recognized as a [[Folk Cultural Property|Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] and part of the "Noto Kiriko Festivals" * [[Ishizaki Hōtō Festival]] - part of the "Noto Kiriko Festivals"
Initially slated for an April 25, 2020, opening, the launch was delayed due to the COVID-19 pandemic.<ref name="Hokkoku"/>
== Features == === Exhibition Hall (Paid Admission)=== The former 1,100-seat main hall has been transformed into a 17-meter-high exhibition space displaying full-scale festival floats and artifacts.<ref>[http://www.ryoko-net.co.jp/?p=78090 Ryoko Shimbun]</ref> Exhibits include: * Seihakusai's "Dekayama" - a new full-scale replica created as a fourth float for permanent display. * Okuma Kabuto's Waku Bata - 16 meters high. * Mukōda Fire Festival’s Ōtaimatsu - represented by a 15-meter banner due to the original’s 30-meter height. * Ishizaki Hōtō’s Daifutō - 13 meters high.
=== Festival Theater and Interactive Zone === The theater features a 16.2-meter-wide screen showing immersive festival footage. Interactive experiences allow visitors to "participate" in festivals through projections and simulations.<ref name="Hokuriku"/>
=== Tourism Information Entrance (Free Zone) === The entrance area features: * Miniature and small-scale exhibits of local floats. * Tourism information and guides. * A gift shop offering festival-themed souvenirs.
=== Additional Facilities === * Meeting rooms and Japanese-style rooms (available for rent). * Cooking practice rooms.
== Visitor Information == * Open: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (last entry at 4:30 PM) * Closed: Second and fourth Wednesdays (or following day if a holiday) and during New Year’s holidays. * Admission: Adults ¥800, children ¥400, infants free. * Free parking available for buses and private vehicles.
== Access == * '''By car''': ~6 minutes from [[Wakura IC]] on the [[Noto Satoyama Kaidō]]. * '''By train''': ~20 minutes on foot or 6 minutes by car from [[:en:Wakura Onsen Station|Wakura Onsen Station]]. * '''By bus''': Immediate access from "Omatsuri Kaikan" bus stop (Hokutetsu Noto Bus).
== Surrounding Attractions == * {{ill|Wakura Onsen|en|Wakura Onsen|WD=Q7961227|lt=Wakura Onsen|1=Wakura Onsen}} * Wakura Onsen Sōyu Public Bath * [[Wakura Showa Museum and Toy Museum]] * [[Isolite Diatomite Memorial Museum]] * [[Yoshihiro Tsujiguchi Art Museum]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official website|https://wakura-omatsuri.com/|Wakura Onsen Omatsuri Hall}}
{{translated page|ja|和倉温泉お祭り会館|version=102277377|comment=Imported full ja history}}
